DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. REBECCA M COLL OF THE QUADRA & COLL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-426-3502 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. KEVIN J. HOLL OF THE GORDON-CREED KELLEY, HOLL & SUGERMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-969-6752 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - Judge Kahn disclosed that Mr. Holl is the spouse of one of his colleague and does not affect his ability to be fair and impartial. No objection was made. The matter was argued. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of duty of common carrier alleged in the complaint filed by Marleen Green is overruled. Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal. 3d 780, which remains good law, held that a public agency could be sued for breach of the duty set forth in Civil Code 2100 without need to show negligence of the agency's employees and that such a claim is independent of any claim set forth in the Government Claims Act, including Government Code 815.2. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711
